db_connection: N/A — this file is derived from Phase 1 code structure, not DB state.

hidden_business_rules.rule: A basket cannot be checked out if it has no items.
Basket lookups by id must succeed or the operation fails fast with a typed exception (not a generic null-ref).
A CatalogItem's Name must be unique — creating a new catalog item with a name that already exists is explicitly rejected via DuplicateException rather than relying on a DB unique constraint error.
hidden_business_rules.evidence: src/PublicApi/CatalogItemEndpoints/CreateCatalogItemEndpoint.cs:47 — `throw new DuplicateException($"A catalogItem with name {request.Name} already exists")`; src/ApplicationCore/Exceptions/DuplicateException.cs
hidden_business_rules.notes: CONFIRMED via spot check — previously UNKNOWN which entity/field this guarded.

hidden_business_rules.rule: Order line items must capture a point-in-time snapshot of product name/price/image rather than a live reference, so historical orders display correctly even if the catalog item is later changed or deleted.
hidden_business_rules.evidence: CatalogItemOrdered owned type (src/ApplicationCore/Entities/OrderAggregate/CatalogItemOrdered.cs) embedded in OrderItem

hidden_business_rules.rule: Inventory reordering: items have AvailableStock, RestockThreshold, MaxStockThreshold and an OnReorder flag — implies an automatic/manual reorder workflow triggers when AvailableStock <= RestockThreshold.
hidden_business_rules.notes: Actual trigger logic (5 calculation-category artifacts reported in extraction summary) not visible in supplied excerpts — Agent 2 should locate the reorder calculation method.

hidden_business_rules.rule: Role-based access control distinguishes at least an 'Administrators' role from regular buyers, gating BlazorAdmin and certain PublicApi/Web endpoints.
hidden_business_rules.evidence: CONFIRMED — src/BlazorShared/Authorization/Constants.cs: `public const string ADMINISTRATORS = "Administrators";`. AuthorizationConstants (src/ApplicationCore/Constants/AuthorizationConstants.cs) holds unrelated auth secrets, not role names — see RC-006 in pii-inventory.json.

hidden_business_rules.rule: Frontend caching layer for catalog lookups (brands/types/items) is implemented via browser localStorage (CacheEntry<T> with a DateCreated timestamp), but the staleness/expiry check itself was not located in this pass.
hidden_business_rules.notes: Storage mechanism now CONFIRMED (localStorage, not IMemoryCache — see RC-004). Exact TTL/expiry comparison logic still UNKNOWN, confidence < 0.7 for that specific detail.

hidden_business_rules.rule: The BlazorAdmin client-side auth state (CustomAuthStateProvider) refreshes/re-validates the cached user against the server at most once every 60 seconds.
hidden_business_rules.notes: NEW finding (RC-005) — a concrete, previously-undocumented TTL value.

hidden_business_rules.rule: RefreshBroadcast mechanism implies that after an admin write (e.g. updating a catalog item), connected Blazor components must be notified to refresh/invalidate their cached view.
hidden_business_rules.notes: Below 0.70 confidence threshold — INFERRED purely from class name, marked UNKNOWN for exact mechanism.

hidden_business_rules.rule: 1 feature flag exists in the codebase controlling some behavior toggle.
hidden_business_rules.notes: UNKNOWN — a spot-check grep for FeatureManagement/IFeatureManager/FeatureFlag found no matches in src/. Could not be located in this pass; remains an open item for Gate G1 / a deeper grep across config files.

change_records.what: Confirmed DuplicateException guards CatalogItem.Name uniqueness on create (PublicApi CreateCatalogItemEndpoint), not Buyer or CatalogBrand/Type as speculated.

Confirmed literal role name string is exactly 'Administrators' (BlazorShared.Authorization.Constants.Roles.ADMINISTRATORS). Also clarified that AuthorizationConstants.cs (ApplicationCore) does not itself contain role names — it holds AUTH_KEY/JWT_SECRET_KEY/DEFAULT_PASSWORD (see RC-006).
Confirmed caching mechanism is browser localStorage via CacheEntry<T> (Value + DateCreated), not IMemoryCache. TTL comparison logic itself still not located.
New finding: BlazorAdmin's CustomAuthStateProvider re-validates cached auth state at most every 60 seconds (UserCacheRefreshInterval).
